title: Gross vs net: how a 28-handicap beats a 5
microFact: Gross score = strokes taken. Net = gross minus handicap. Net is how amateurs of any level compete fairly.
lesson:
Every competition score exists twice:

GROSS: the actual strokes you took. 103 shots = 103 gross.
NET: gross MINUS your course handicap. 103 with a 30 handicap = net 73 — which beats a 10-handicapper's 85 (net 75).

This is golf's genius: the only mainstream sport where a beginner and an expert can play a genuinely fair, meaningful match. Handicaps convert everyone's game to "performance versus YOUR OWN expected level."

Most club events award BOTH prizes — "gross winner" (usually the best raw golfer) and "net winner" (whoever beat their own standard by most). As a new player with an honest handicap, you can win net prizes from day one — and new players who play slightly better than their fresh handicap often do. Nobody resents a net win off an honest cap; everyone resents one off a padded cap (byte 41).

When results are read at dinner: "net 68 off 27" means gross 95 by a 27-handicapper. You'll hear appreciative murmurs — that's a fine round.

quiz.question: You shoot 104 gross with a course handicap of 29. Your net score?
